reasoning: The Bank Term Funding Program (BTFP) ceased extending new loans on March 11, 2024, and the maximum term for any loan was one year. Consequently, all loans matured by March 11, 2025. Recent FRED data shows that the balance reached 0.0 on March 12, 2025, and has remained at 0.0 in subsequent weeks (March 19 and March 26). The value on March 30, 2025, will definitively be 0.0. For the balance to increase above 0.0 by any of the resolution dates, the Federal Reserve would have to restart the BTFP and issue new loans. This is highly improbable because the program was a temporary emergency measure tailored to the March 2023 banking stress. Furthermore, the BTFP had known issues (such as the arbitrage opportunity that the Fed had to patch by raising rates in Jan 2024), so if another crisis occurred, the Fed would almost certainly use a different facility or standard discount window lending rather than reopening the exact same program with the exact same FRED series. Given these factors, the probability of the balance increasing above 0.0 is very low, near the 5% minimum allowed, stepping up only slightly for the furthest horizon.
